_comment: === INTERNAL REFINERY EDGES: refinery_in → refinery_out (capacity = processing limit) ===

_source: Reliance Industries 68.2 MMTPA × 20,000 = 1,364,000 bbl/day. Refinery processing cost (not crude cost): ~$3/bbl [ESTIMATED] representative of complex refinery operating cost.
PPAC: 15.0 MMTPA = 300,000 bbl/day.

_comment: === MIDDLE EAST SOURCES → STRAIT OF HORMUZ ===

_source: Basrah to Hormuz transit: ~1 day. Freight from Basrah terminal to Hormuz entrance is minimal — essentially a pipeline to offshore anchorage. Cost represents terminal fees [ESTIMATED].
Ras Tanura/Ju'aymah terminals to Hormuz: ~1 day.
Ruwais/Jebel Ali to Hormuz: ~1 day.
Mina Abdulla/Shuaiba to Hormuz: ~1 day.

_comment: === HORMUZ → WEST COAST INDIAN REFINERIES (direct, via Arabian Sea) ===

_source: Hormuz to Jamnagar (Sikka terminal, Gujarat): ~6 days. Freight rate $1.50/bbl [ESTIMATED based on Baltic Dirty Tanker Index, VLCC Ras Tanura-West India route]. Capacity set to Jamnagar's intake + buffer; binding constraint is the internal refinery edge.
Hormuz to Mangalore (Karnataka, west coast): ~8 days. Slightly higher cost due to longer transit south.
Hormuz to Kochi (Kerala, SPM facility): ~9 days.

_comment: === HORMUZ → MALACCA (for east coast routing of Middle East crude) ===

_source: Hormuz to Malacca Strait (through Indian Ocean, around tip of Sri Lanka): ~7 days. Used when routing Gulf crude to east coast refineries (Paradip, Vizag). Cost lower than direct west-coast delivery as tankers avoid port congestion.
Malacca to Paradip Port (Odisha): ~8 days across Bay of Bengal. Capacity slightly above refinery capacity to avoid artificial bottleneck here.
Malacca to Visakhapatnam Port (Andhra Pradesh): ~7 days. Slightly closer to Malacca than Paradip.
Russia ESPO: Kozmino (Pacific) to Malacca ~14 days. Path regenerated via searoute from the correct Pacific origin.
Russia Urals: Novorossiysk (Black Sea) via Med/Suez/Red Sea to Bab-el-Mandeb ~20 days. Path regenerated via searoute.

_comment: === BAB-EL-MANDEB → SUEZ CANAL (serial connection for westbound routes) ===

_source: Bab-el-Mandeb to Suez Canal transit: ~2 days (Red Sea crossing). Canal fees approx. $0.60/bbl for oil tankers [ESTIMATED based on Suez Canal Authority published tariffs for Suezmax class].

_comment: === SUEZ → INDIAN REFINERIES (via Arabian Sea, southbound exit from canal) ===

_source: Suez exit to Jamnagar via Arabian Sea: ~8 days. Higher cost than Hormuz route ($2.00 vs $1.50/bbl) reflecting Suez canal fees and longer transit. Capacity constrained: primarily Russia Urals + Nigeria SWEET for Jamnagar.
Suez to Mangalore: ~10 days. $2.20/bbl [ESTIMATED].
Suez to Kochi: ~11 days. Southernmost west coast refinery, longest transit from Suez.
Suez to Malacca (for routing Russia Urals to east coast refineries via Indian Ocean): ~14 days.
Nigeria (Bonny terminal) to Bab-el-Mandeb via Gulf of Guinea, around Horn of Africa or via Suez: ~15 days. Primary route before Houthi attacks. $2.50/bbl [ESTIMATED, West Africa-India Aframax rate].

Nigeria to Cape of Good Hope: ~22 days (bypass route, actively used in 2024 due to Houthi attacks). $3.80/bbl [ESTIMATED, adds ~$1.30/bbl over primary route]. EIA 2024 data confirms this rerouting actually happened.
US Gulf Coast (Houston) to Cape of Good Hope: ~28 days (VLCC Atlantic crossing). $4.50/bbl [ESTIMATED, long-haul VLCC rate]. US crude to India is a relatively recent and growing trade flow.

_comment: === CAPE OF GOOD HOPE → INDIAN REFINERIES (bypass route, higher cost) ===

_source: Cape of Good Hope to Jamnagar (Gujarat west coast): ~12 days further transit after rounding the cape. $3.00/bbl from Cape to Jamnagar [ESTIMATED]. Capacity 400,000: constrained by port and vessel scheduling availability, not the route per se. EIA confirmed 9.3 Mb/d globally rerouted via Cape in 2024; India's share estimated.
Cape to Mangalore: ~13 days. $3.20/bbl. Capacity 250,000 [ESTIMATED].
Cape to Kochi: ~14 days. $3.20/bbl.
Cape to Malacca Strait (for east coast refineries via Cape bypass): ~15 days across Indian Ocean.

ISPRL Vizag to HPCL Vizag refinery: direct pipeline link (emergency release). Max flow = SPR max discharge 85,000 bbl/day [ESTIMATED]. High cost $8.00/bbl ensures solver only uses SPR when cheaper routes cannot meet demand — serves as last-resort cost signal, not an economic value.
ISPRL Mangalore to MRPL Mangalore: pipeline link. 95,000 bbl/day [ESTIMATED].
ISPRL Padur to BPCL Kochi: pipeline (Padur is ~200km north of Kochi, pipeline exists per MoPNG). 160,000 bbl/day [ESTIMATED].
ISPRL Padur can also feed MRPL Mangalore (closer facility). Shared capacity with Kochi pipe; total Padur discharge capped at 160,000 bbl/day across both edges combined.
Virtual edge.

Direct Oman (Mina Al Fahal) -> Jamnagar (Reliance) — Inlet (no chokepoint transit). Distance 1335 km via searoute; cost/time derived from the 2.91 days/1000km, $0.51/1000km long-haul calibration (see script header).

Direct Oman (Mina Al Fahal) -> Mangalore (MRPL) — Inlet (no chokepoint transit). Distance 2383 km via searoute; cost/time derived from the 2.91 days/1000km, $0.51/1000km long-haul calibration (see script header).
